content: Hey, I'm so excited for the weekend. I get to volunteer at the animal shelter and then work on my new music set for the upcoming festival on August 20th.
That sounds like a great way to spend your weekend! What kind of music do you usually produce and perform?
I'm really into electronic dance music, but I've been experimenting with incorporating piano into my sets. I've been taking lessons with my teacher, Rachel, for about 6 months now.
Piano can add such a unique element to electronic music. Have you performed at any notable venues or events recently?

Actually, I just got back from a gig in Los Angeles and I'm heading to Chicago for another show on September 1st. I love traveling for music, but it's also nice to have some downtime to focus on my home brewing beer hobby.
That's really cool. Home brewing can be such a fun and rewarding hobby. What kind of beers do you like to brew?
I'm really into IPAs and pale ales. I've been experimenting with different hops and yeast strains to create unique flavor profiles. My friend, Alex, has been helping me out with the brewing process.
IPAs and pale ales are always popular choices. If you don't mind me asking, how did you get into music production and DJing?

I've been into music my whole life, but I started producing and DJing about 10 years ago. I used to run a small record label with my business partner, Jamie, but now I focus more on my own music and performing.
That's really impressive. It's great that you've been able to turn your passion into a career. Do you have any upcoming marathons or running events that you're training for?

Yeah, I'm actually training for the New York City Marathon on November 6th. It'll be my 5th marathon, but I'm still really nervous about it. I've been trying to balance my training with my music and volunteering schedule.
Good luck with your training! It's great that you're prioritizing your physical and mental health. Maybe I can help you come up with a training plan or provide some tips for balancing your schedule?
